qualifying_vulnerability_assessments.title: Hardcoded HMAC key in Cookie class allows forging signed cookies
qualifying_vulnerability_assessments.advisory_match_reason: The finding describes a hardcoded HMAC key in the Cookie class allowing cookie forgery. The advisory describes missing authorization checks for pages.access/list and files.access/list permissions. While both involve authentication/authorization aspects, the root causes are different: hardcoded cryptographic key vs missing permission enforcement checks. The advisory is about permission-based access control failures, not cryptographic key management.

qualifying_vulnerability_assessments.title: Unauthenticated system information disclosure via GET /api/system
qualifying_vulnerability_assessments.advisory_match_reason: The finding describes unauthenticated information disclosure via GET /api/system endpoint. The advisory describes authorization bypass for authenticated users with restricted pages/files permissions. The finding involves unauthenticated access to system info, while the advisory requires authenticated users with specific restricted permissions. The vulnerable endpoints, permission checks, and impact are fundamentally different.

target_advisory_judgement.reason: None of the qualifying findings match the advisory-described vulnerability. The advisory describes missing authorization checks for pages.access/list and files.access/list permissions in Kirby CMS Panel and REST API, allowing authenticated users with restricted permissions to access/list pages and files they shouldn't see. The two qualifying findings are: (1) hardcoded cookie HMAC key allowing cookie forgery, and (2) unauthenticated system information disclosure. These are different vulnerability classes with different root causes, affecting different components, and requiring different preconditions than the authorization bypass described in the advisory.
evidence: Advisory GHSA-85x2-r8xv-ww8c describes authorization bypass for pages.access/list and files.access/list permissions affecting authenticated users with restricted permissions.
Finding 'hardcoded-cookie-hmac-key' describes cryptographic failure with hardcoded HMAC key - different from permission enforcement bypass.
Finding 'unauth-system-info-disclosure' describes unauthenticated information disclosure - requires no authentication while advisory requires authenticated users with specific restricted permissions.
